basic_details.job_title: MPESB Agriculture Extension Officer Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 2784 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Krishi Vistar Adhikari (Agriculture Extension Officer) – Post Code 01
basic_details.department: Directorate of Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development, Madhya Pradesh, Bhopal
basic_details.short_information: The Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board (formerly MP Vyapam/PEB) will conduct the Group-2 Sub Group-1 combined recruitment test 2026 for a single post: Krishi Vistar Adhikari (Agriculture Extension Officer). The key highlights are summarised below.

application_fee.details: MP Online portal fee: Rs. 60 if the form is filled through a kiosk, or Rs. 20 if filled through a registered citizen user login. Correction fee: Rs. 20 per correction, plus a portal fee of Rs. 40 for each correction made during the correction window. The fee can be paid through credit card, debit card, net banking or at an authorised MP Online kiosk.

age_limit.details: The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years . The maximum age limit is calculated as on 1 January 2026 and varies by category as shown below. | Category Maximum Age Unreserved (Open direct recruitment) 40 years MP domicile SC / ST / OBC candidates 45 years Women candidates of MP (reserved and unreserved) 45 years MP Government / Corporation / Board employees, Home Guards, Divyangjan (PwD) 45 years EWS candidates 40 years (no age relaxation for EWS) Contract (Samvida) employees and Rule-14 priority categories Up to 55 years (as per applicable relaxation rules)

qualification.details: The educational qualification for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026 is a Graduate degree (Bachelor's degree) in Agriculture or Horticulture from a recognised university. | The qualification must be fully completed on or before the date of submitting the online application. Candidates acquiring the degree after the application date will not be considered eligible. The marksheet number, marks obtained and maximum marks of the qualifying degree must be entered in the online form. | The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years. The maximum age limit is calculated as on 1 January 2026 and varies by category as shown below. | The candidate must be a citizen of India (Nepal citizens may be eligible subject to the certificate required under MP Civil Services Recruitment Rules, 1961). Live registration in an Employment Office of Madhya Pradesh is mandatory for all applicants. Candidates who are not domiciles of Madhya Pradesh can apply only against Unreserved (Open) posts and will not get any fee concession, reservation or age relaxation. A candidate having more than two children, where one child is born on or after 26 January 2001, is not eligible for appointment. Candidates who married before the legal age (18 years for women, 21 years for men) are not eligible for appointment. Since the post is an Executive (Karyapalik) post, Baiga, Sahariya/Saharia and Bharia special primitive tribe candidates do not get exam exemption; they must apply online and appear in the exam as ST candidates. Aadhaar registration is mandatory, and the Aadhaar must be unlocked at the time of biometric verification.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be appointed in the pay scale of Rs. 25,300 – 80,500 (Class III). As per the MP General Administration Department circular dated 12 December 2019, a stipend is paid during the probation period instead of the full pay scale: | First year: 70% of the minimum of the pay scale (approximately Rs. 17,710 per month) Second year: 80% of the minimum of the pay scale (approximately Rs. 20,240 per month) Third year: 90% of the minimum of the pay scale (approximately Rs. 22,770 per month) | After successful completion of the probation period, the regular pay in the scale of Rs. 25,300 – 80,500, along with admissible allowances, is paid.

selection_process: The selection for the Agriculture Extension Officer post will be made through a single online written exam followed by document verification. There is no interview.
Online Written Exam (CBT): Objective-type paper of 200 marks conducted by MPESB. Normalization: If the exam is held in multiple shifts

results are prepared using the Normalised Equi-Percentile (NEP) scaling technique. Merit List and Waiting List: MPESB prepares a post-wise merit list; a waiting list of 15% of the total posts remains valid for one year or until the next exam result
whichever is earlier. Document Verification: Certificates are verified by the Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development Department
not by MPESB. Appointment: Appointment orders are to be issued within a maximum of three months from the date of the selection list.
Unreserved (UR): 50% marks SC / ST / OBC / EWS / Divyangjan: 40% marks (after 10% relaxation)
EWS candidates get the 10% relaxation in qualifying marks
but their selection is made on merit from the EWS list.

pattern_table.Subjects: General Knowledge, General Hindi, General English, General Maths, General Reasoning Ability, General Science, General Computer Knowledge

exam_pattern.details: The exam consists of a single question paper of 200 marks with two parts, to be solved in 3 hours. | Questions are objective (MCQ) type with four options each, in Hindi and English medium. Each correct answer carries 1 mark. Negative marking applies: 0.25 marks are deducted for every wrong answer, as per clause 3.8 of the official rulebook. After the exam, candidates must confirm their RAW score (number of correct answers) displayed on the computer screen before submitting. Objections against questions can be filed online within three days of the objection link going live, with a fee of Rs. 150 per question. There is no provision for re-evaluation or re-totalling after the result is declared.

syllabus.details: General Knowledge General Hindi General English General Maths General Reasoning Ability General Science General Computer Knowledge | General Agriculture: Agriculture and its importance in the national economy. Agronomy: Major crops of MP, agro-techniques of major field crops of MP, farming systems and sustainable agriculture, introductory agro-meteorology, agro-climatic and agro-ecological zones. Soil Science: Soil composition and its role in crop production; physical, chemical and biological properties of soil; essential plant nutrients, their functions and dynamics; integrated nutrient management; problem soils and their management. Soil and Water Conservation: Watershed management. Plant Physiology: Absorption, translocation and metabolism of nutrients; photosynthesis and respiration; growth and development; growth regulators. Crop Improvement: Elements of genetics and plant breeding as applied to crop improvement. Horticulture: Package of practices for important fruits, vegetables, spices and flowering plants; nursery management and propagation methods; physiological disorders and their management; post-harvest management and value addition. Plant Protection: Important insect pests and diseases and their management; integrated pest and disease management; spray equipment selection and maintenance; rodent management; pesticide safety precautions. Agricultural Economics: Principles of economics applied to agriculture; farm planning and resource management; farming systems; marketing of agricultural produce and regulated markets in MP (including e-Chaupal); pricing of agricultural produce. Agricultural Extension Education: Philosophy, objectives and principles of extension; extension organisations at state, district and block levels; communication methods; role of farmers' organisations; role and importance of training; important rural development programmes in India.

how_to_apply: Only online applications are accepted; offline applications will be rejected. Follow these steps to apply between 3 July and 17 July 2026:
Visit the official MPESB website esb.mp.gov.in or the MP Online portal mponline.gov.in.
Open the application link for "Group-2 Sub Group-1 Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment Test 2026".
Read the rulebook and eligibility conditions carefully before starting the form.
Fill in personal details, Aadhaar number/VID, permanent body identification marks, photo ID details, and educational qualification details (marksheet number, marks obtained and maximum marks).
Upload the photograph, signature and handwriting sample in the prescribed JPG format.
Select four exam city preferences in order of priority.
Pay the application fee through credit card, debit card, net banking or an authorised MP Online kiosk.
Submit the form, note down the application number, and keep a printout of the application and fee receipt safely. The application number is required to download the admit card.
important_instructions: Aadhaar registration is compulsory, and multi-level Aadhaar-enabled biometric verification will be done at the exam centre. Keep your Aadhaar biometrics unlocked on the exam day; otherwise, entry to the exam will be denied. Entry to the exam centre is allowed only up to the reporting time. Latecomers will not be admitted. Mobile phones, calculators, log tables, digital watches, sunglasses, whitener, and all electronic devices are strictly prohibited inside the exam hall. Violations are treated as Unfair Means (UFM) cases. Candidates cannot leave the exam hall before the exam ends. The educational qualification must be complete on or before the date of applying; final-year or result-awaited candidates are not eligible. Eligibility in the online exam is purely provisional; certificates are verified by the department during the appointment process. Any wrong information or impersonation leads to cancellation of candidature, and impersonation cases also invite an FIR and legal action. No correction of any kind is allowed after the correction window closes on 22 July 2026, and no correction is entertained after the admit card is issued. Objections to questions can be submitted only online, within three days of the objection link opening, at Rs. 150 per question. Travel expense reimbursement (for eligible MP domicile SC / ST / OBC and PwD candidates) is paid by the department through e-payment; submit bank details in Proforma-1 with the required enclosures. For application-related help, contact the MP Online helpdesk at 0755-6720200 or the MPESB toll-free number 18002337899. Complaints can be emailed to [email protected].

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: The Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board (formerly MP Vyapam/PEB) will conduct the Group-2 Sub Group-1 combined recruitment test 2026 for a single post: Krishi Vistar Adhikari (Agriculture Extension Officer). The key highlights are summarised below.

rows.details: Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board (MPESB), Bhopal
Group-2 Sub Group-1 Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment Exam 2026
Krishi Vistar Adhikari (Agriculture Extension Officer) – Post Code 01
Directorate of Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development, Madhya Pradesh, Bhopal

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari 2026 Important Dates
content_sections.text: The last date to apply online for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026 is 17 July 2026 , and corrections in the application form can be made up to 22 July 2026. Candidates arriving after the reporting time will not be allowed to enter the exam centre.

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Vacancy 2026 Details
content_sections.text: A total of 2784 vacancies of Krishi Vistar Adhikari have been advertised by the Directorate of Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development, Madhya Pradesh. The category-wise distribution is given below. 168 posts are horizontally reserved for Divyangjan (PwD) candidates: 42 each for VH, HH, LD and MD categories. These posts are included within the Open posts of each category. 35% horizontal reservation applies for women and 10% for ex-servicemen in Class III posts, as reflected in the table above. Since the 27% OBC reservation matter is sub judice in the Hon'ble High Court, the rulebook also publishes a main table of 2410 posts (with OBC at 14%) and a provisional table of 374 posts.

content_sections.heading: MPESB Agriculture Extension Officer Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: The educational qualification for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026 is a Graduate degree (Bachelor's degree) in Agriculture or Horticulture from a recognised university. The qualification must be fully completed on or before the date of submitting the online application. Candidates acquiring the degree after the application date will not be considered eligible. The marksheet number, marks obtained and maximum marks of the qualifying degree must be entered in the online form. The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years . The maximum age limit is calculated as on 1 January 2026 and varies by category as shown below. The candidate must be a citizen of India (Nepal citizens may be eligible subject to the certificate required under MP Civil Services Recruitment Rules, 1961). Live registration in an Employment Office of Madhya Pradesh is mandatory for all applicants. Candidates who are not domiciles of Madhya Pradesh can apply only against Unreserved (Open) posts and will not get any fee concession, reservation or age relaxation. A candidate having more than two children, where one child is born on or after 26 January 2001, is not eligible for appointment. Candidates who married before the legal age (18 years for women, 21 years for men) are not eligible for appointment. Since the post is an Executive (Karyapalik) post, Baiga, Sahariya/Saharia and Bharia special primitive tribe candidates do not get exam exemption; they must apply online and appear in the exam as ST candidates. Aadhaar registration is mandatory, and the Aadhaar must be unlocked at the time of biometric verification.

Women candidates of MP (reserved and unreserved)
MP Government / Corporation / Board employees, Home Guards, Divyangjan (PwD)
Contract (Samvida) employees and Rule-14 priority categories
rows.maximum_age: Up to 55 years (as per applicable relaxation rules)

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Salary 2026
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be appointed in the pay scale of Rs. 25,300 – 80,500 (Class III) . As per the MP General Administration Department circular dated 12 December 2019, a stipend is paid during the probation period instead of the full pay scale: First year: 70% of the minimum of the pay scale (approximately Rs. 17,710 per month) Second year: 80% of the minimum of the pay scale (approximately Rs. 20,240 per month) Third year: 90% of the minimum of the pay scale (approximately Rs. 22,770 per month) After successful completion of the probation period, the regular pay in the scale of Rs. 25,300 – 80,500, along with admissible allowances, is paid.

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: MP Online portal fee: Rs. 60 if the form is filled through a kiosk, or Rs. 20 if filled through a registered citizen user login. Correction fee: Rs. 20 per correction, plus a portal fee of Rs. 40 for each correction made during the correction window. The fee can be paid through credit card, debit card, net banking or at an authorised MP Online kiosk.

rows.category: SC / ST / OBC / EWS / Divyangjan (MP domicile only)

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: The selection for the Agriculture Extension Officer post will be made through a single online written exam followed by document verification. There is no interview. Online Written Exam (CBT): Objective-type paper of 200 marks conducted by MPESB. Normalization: If the exam is held in multiple shifts, results are prepared using the Normalised Equi-Percentile (NEP) scaling technique. Merit List and Waiting List: MPESB prepares a post-wise merit list; a waiting list of 15% of the total posts remains valid for one year or until the next exam result, whichever is earlier. Document Verification: Certificates are verified by the Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development Department, not by MPESB. Appointment: Appointment orders are to be issued within a maximum of three months from the date of the selection list. Unreserved (UR): 50% marks SC / ST / OBC / EWS / Divyangjan: 40% marks (after 10% relaxation) EWS candidates get the 10% relaxation in qualifying marks, but their selection is made on merit from the EWS list.

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Exam Pattern 2026
content_sections.text: The exam consists of a single question paper of 200 marks with two parts, to be solved in 3 hours. Questions are objective (MCQ) type with four options each, in Hindi and English medium. Each correct answer carries 1 mark. Negative marking applies: 0.25 marks are deducted for every wrong answer , as per clause 3.8 of the official rulebook. After the exam, candidates must confirm their RAW score (number of correct answers) displayed on the computer screen before submitting. Objections against questions can be filed online within three days of the objection link going live, with a fee of Rs. 150 per question. There is no provision for re-evaluation or re-totalling after the result is declared.

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Syllabus 2026
content_sections.text: General Knowledge General Hindi General English General Maths General Reasoning Ability General Science General Computer Knowledge General Agriculture: Agriculture and its importance in the national economy. Agronomy: Major crops of MP, agro-techniques of major field crops of MP, farming systems and sustainable agriculture, introductory agro-meteorology, agro-climatic and agro-ecological zones. Soil Science: Soil composition and its role in crop production; physical, chemical and biological properties of soil; essential plant nutrients, their functions and dynamics; integrated nutrient management; problem soils and their management. Soil and Water Conservation: Watershed management. Plant Physiology: Absorption, translocation and metabolism of nutrients; photosynthesis and respiration; growth and development; growth regulators. Crop Improvement: Elements of genetics and plant breeding as applied to crop improvement. Horticulture: Package of practices for important fruits, vegetables, spices and flowering plants; nursery management and propagation methods; physiological disorders and their management; post-harvest management and value addition. Plant Protection: Important insect pests and diseases and their management; integrated pest and disease management; spray equipment selection and maintenance; rodent management; pesticide safety precautions. Agricultural Economics: Principles of economics applied to agriculture; farm planning and resource management; farming systems; marketing of agricultural produce and regulated markets in MP (including e-Chaupal); pricing of agricultural produce. Agricultural Extension Education: Philosophy, objectives and principles of extension; extension organisations at state, district and block levels; communication methods; role of farmers' organisations; role and importance of training; important rural development programmes in India.

content_sections.heading: MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Exam Cities 2026
content_sections.text: Applicants must select four exam city preferences while filling the online form. Exam centres are allotted randomly, subject to seat availability, and MPESB's decision on centre allotment is final. The written exam is proposed to be held in the following 11 cities: Bhopal Indore Jabalpur Khandwa Neemuch Ratlam Rewa Sagar Satna Sidhi Ujjain

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Recent colour photograph (white background, not older than three months, with the candidate's name and date of photograph printed on it, both ears clearly visible) Scanned signature (short signatures, signatures in capital letters, or multiple signatures are not accepted) Handwriting sample (two lines in the candidate's own handwriting, in the prescribed format) Class 8th / 10th / 12th marksheet as proof of date of birth Caste / category certificate (SC / ST / OBC / EWS) issued by the competent MP authority, where applicable Disability certificate for Divyangjan candidates, issued by the competent authority Contract service certificate (minimum five years of continuous samvida service) for contract employee quota claimants Bonus-mark certificates such as the NCC 'C' certificate, where applicable Admit card (Test Admit Card – TAC) downloaded from esb.mp.gov.in, with Part 2 entries duly filled Original photo ID: Voter ID, PAN Card, Aadhaar Card, Driving Licence or Passport (an e-Aadhaar printout is valid only if verified by UIDAI) Black ballpoint pen

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Only online applications are accepted; offline applications will be rejected. Follow these steps to apply between 3 July and 17 July 2026: Visit the official MPESB website esb.mp.gov.in or the MP Online portal mponline.gov.in. Open the application link for "Group-2 Sub Group-1 Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment Test 2026". Read the rulebook and eligibility conditions carefully before starting the form. Fill in personal details, Aadhaar number/VID, permanent body identification marks, photo ID details, and educational qualification details (marksheet number, marks obtained and maximum marks). Upload the photograph, signature and handwriting sample in the prescribed JPG format. Select four exam city preferences in order of priority. Pay the application fee through credit card, debit card, net banking or an authorised MP Online kiosk. Submit the form, note down the application number, and keep a printout of the application and fee receipt safely. The application number is required to download the admit card.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for Applicants
content_sections.text: Aadhaar registration is compulsory, and multi-level Aadhaar-enabled biometric verification will be done at the exam centre. Keep your Aadhaar biometrics unlocked on the exam day; otherwise, entry to the exam will be denied. Entry to the exam centre is allowed only up to the reporting time. Latecomers will not be admitted. Mobile phones, calculators, log tables, digital watches, sunglasses, whitener, and all electronic devices are strictly prohibited inside the exam hall. Violations are treated as Unfair Means (UFM) cases. Candidates cannot leave the exam hall before the exam ends. The educational qualification must be complete on or before the date of applying; final-year or result-awaited candidates are not eligible. Eligibility in the online exam is purely provisional; certificates are verified by the department during the appointment process. Any wrong information or impersonation leads to cancellation of candidature, and impersonation cases also invite an FIR and legal action. No correction of any kind is allowed after the correction window closes on 22 July 2026, and no correction is entertained after the admit card is issued. Objections to questions can be submitted only online, within three days of the objection link opening, at Rs. 150 per question. Travel expense reimbursement (for eligible MP domicile SC / ST / OBC and PwD candidates) is paid by the department through e-payment; submit bank details in Proforma-1 with the required enclosures. For application-related help, contact the MP Online helpdesk at 0755-6720200 or the MPESB toll-free number 18002337899. Complaints can be emailed to [email protected] .

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 17 July 2026. Corrections in the submitted form can be made until 22 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 2784 vacancies in total: UR 1126, EWS 444, SC 316, ST 122 and OBC 776, in the Directorate of Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development, MP.

faq.question: Q3. What is the qualification for the MP Agriculture Extension Officer post?
faq.answer: A Bachelor's degree in Agriculture or Horticulture from a recognised university, completed on or before the date of applying online.

faq.question: Q4. When is the MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari exam 2026?
faq.answer: The online exam starts from 2 August 2026 (Sunday) in two shifts: 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM and 2:30 PM to 5:30 PM.

faq.question: Q5. Is there negative marking in the MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari exam?
faq.answer: Yes. As per clause 3.8 of the official rulebook, 1 mark is awarded for each correct answer and 0.25 marks are deducted for each wrong answer.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary of an MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari?
faq.answer: The pay scale is Rs. 25,300 to Rs. 80,500. During probation, a stipend of 70%, 80% and 90% of the minimum pay is paid in the first, second and third years respectively, after which the full pay scale applies.

faq.question: Q7. Can candidates from other states apply for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, but only against Unreserved (Open) posts. Non-MP candidates must pay the Rs. 500 fee and do not get any reservation or age relaxation.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for MPESB Agriculture Extension Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 40 years as on 1 January 2026. MP domicile SC / ST / OBC candidates, women, government employees and PwD candidates get relaxation up to 45 years. EWS candidates get no age relaxation.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based only on the 200-mark online written exam, followed by document verification by the department. There is no interview or physical test for this post.

faq.question: Q10. What is the application fee for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: Rs. 500 for Unreserved candidates and Rs. 250 for MP domicile SC / ST / OBC / EWS / PwD candidates, plus an MP Online portal fee of Rs. 60 (kiosk) or Rs. 20 (citizen login).

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for MPESB Krishi Vistar Adhikari Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 17 July 2026. Corrections in the submitted form can be made until 22 July 2026.
